activation_results.reason: Input matches the skill's described purpose verbatim.
Verb swap from Audit to Review preserves the canonical IaC audit intent.
User invokes the skill by its exact name.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to Terraform/IaC auditing.
Meta question about what skills are is out of scope for an IaC audit.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative uses neutral/AusE forms; none of the banned US spellings appear.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Risk Register cites modules/vpc/main.tf:42, environments/prod/backend.tf:1, modules/rds/main.tf:18 with CRITICAL/HIGH severity bands.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "Executive Summary", "Module Dependency Graph"
criterion_results.evidence: Both headings present (## Executive Summary at line 67, ## Module Dependency Graph at line 78).

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Concrete targets named: acme-infra, environments/prod, environments/staging, modules/vpc, modules/rds.

judge_result.notes: All four judge criteria are satisfied: spelling is AusE/neutral with no banned US forms, findings carry file:line evidence with severity categorisation, both canonical sections (Executive Summary, Module Dependency Graph) are present, and concrete module paths replace placeholders throughout. Activation classification matches expectations across all five cases.
